title_en: Elon Musk Is Out to Rule Space. Can Anyone Stop Him?
title_zh: 埃隆·马斯克想征服太空。有人能阻止他吗？
rubric_en: With SpaceX and Starlink, Elon Musk controls more than half the world’s rocket launches and thousands of internet satellites. That amounts to immense geopolitical power.
rubric_zh: 凭借 SpaceX 和 Starlink，埃隆·马斯克控制了世界上超过一半的火箭发射和数以千计的互联网卫星。这等同于巨大的地缘政治力量。

paragraphs.en: Just off the Jimmy Buffett Memorial Highway, the hotel’s rooftop bar is open late. The bartender passes out shots and turns Ozzy up. It’s 11:37 pm on a hot July night in Cape Canaveral, Florida, when our heads all swivel in the same direction. A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket takes off, its orange plume glowing bright, about 12 miles due north up the Banana River. The “Iron Man” riff starts to blast.
paragraphs.zh: 走出吉米·巴菲特纪念公路后，酒店的屋顶酒吧一直开到很晚。调酒师给我们端来一杯杯酒，并把《铁人》这首歌调得很响。那是七月一个炎热夜晚的晚上11点37分，在美国佛罗里达州卡纳维拉尔角，大家都把头转向同一方向。一枚 SpaceX 的猎鹰9号火箭正在起飞，橘色火焰尾流格外明亮，朝向北面大约12英里外的香蕉河方向。那段“Iron Man”的吉他riff开始轰鸣。

paragraphs.en: It’s fun for the couple dozen of us there. When we hear the thud of the sonic boom, most everyone lets out some kind of hoot. But for Elon Musk, it’s just another Tuesday. This is SpaceX’s 95th launch of the year, one nearly every other day. That’s more liftoffs than the rest of the world gets into space, combined.
paragraphs.zh: 对我们这二十多人来说这是很有趣的。当我们听见音爆的闷响，几乎所有人都会发出一声欢呼。但对埃隆·马斯克来说，这只是又一个周二。这是今年 SpaceX 的第95次发射，几乎是两天一发。这比全世界其他国家和机构加起来一年发射的次数都还要多。

paragraphs.en: For our politics issue, WIRED examines the state of tech’s influence on governmental power—and the people who will change everything in the future.
paragraphs.zh: 在这期政治专题中，《连线》杂志审视了技术对政府权力的影响现状，以及那些将改变未来的一群人。

paragraphs.en: On this particular night, this Falcon 9 took 28 Starlink internet satellites to orbit. Starlink, of course, is another Musk space venture that dominates its competitors. His constellation has more than 8,000 satellites; its closest competitor, Eutelsat’s OneWeb, has about 630 satellites, each supplying less than 1/10th the bandwidth of a Starlink. Amazon is going all in on its own service, called Project Kuiper and led by SpaceX’s former satellite chief. The terms of Kuiper’s license from the feds require it to get 1,600 satellites into orbit by the middle of next year. So far, the Amazon constellation has 102.
paragraphs.zh: 就在这一晚，这枚猎鹰9号把28颗 Starlink 互联网卫星送入轨道。Starlink 当然是马斯克的另一项太空业务，而且远远领先竞争对手。该星座已有超过8,000颗卫星；它的最接近竞争对手是Eutelsat旗下的 OneWeb，大约有630颗卫星，每颗的带宽供应量不足 Starlink 的十分之一。亚马逊则正在全力押注自己的服务——Kuiper 计划，由 SpaceX 的前卫星主管领导。联邦政府给 Kuiper 的许可要求其在明年年中前发射1600颗卫星。到目前为止，亚马逊的星座只有102颗卫星。

paragraphs.en: It’s hard to quantify, even with those numbers, the geopolitical power that Musk now commands by way of his two space businesses. When Starlink went down for a couple of hours in late July, troops on both sides of the Russia-Ukraine conflict had trouble connecting with their drones—and one another. “Everyone thought it was purely on the front lines, until reports started coming in that he had fallen all over the world,” one officer stationed near the city of Kupiansk, along the Oskil River in eastern Ukraine, texts me. That’s how central Musk is to modern warfare. Two days after the launch I watched from the hotel roof, another Falcon 9 took off from Cape Canaveral, this one carrying four astronauts aboard a Dragon capsule to the International Space Station. SpaceX’s Dragon is currently America’s only way to get humans into space, as Musk reminded his onetime ally Donald Trump when the president threatened Musk’s government contracts.
paragraphs.zh: 仅凭这些数字也很难量化马斯克现在借助他的两家航天业务所掌握的地缘政治力量。2026年7月下旬，Starlink 断线了几个小时后，俄罗斯乌克兰冲突双方的部队都很难与其无人机以及彼此保持通信。“最初所有人都以为只是前线现场出现了问题，直到报告开始涌来，显示它已经在世界各地掉线，”一名驻扎在乌克兰东部奥斯基尔河边库皮扬斯克附近的军官在给我发的短信中说。这正说明现代战争中马斯克是多么关键。两天后，我在酒店楼顶看到另一枚猎鹰9号升空，它搭载了四名宇航员在龙飞船里飞往国际空间站。正如马斯克在特朗普总统威胁要取消其政府合同时提醒当年的盟友时所说，SpaceX 的龙飞船目前是美国把人类送入太空的唯一途径。

paragraphs.en: Now, Musk has a chance to leverage his two dominant positions into a third. For the first time in decades, America is openly working on the weaponization of space, in response to what the Pentagon claims are threats from Russia and China. The Pentagon is investing in spacecraft that can fly up to other countries’ satellites and attack. Separately, the president has pledged $175 billion for a program that could eventually entail hundreds and hundreds of orbiting interceptors and even more communications satellites to allow them to work together.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，马斯克有机会把他那两个核心地位转化为第三个支点。几十年来，美国首次公开把武器化太空列入行动议程，这是对五角大楼所称来自俄罗斯和中国威胁的回应。五角大楼正在投资研发可飞抵他国卫星并实施打击的太空器。与此同时，总统已承诺投入1750亿美元，用于一项可能最终包括数百乃至上千个在轨拦截器、以及更多通信卫星，以实现它们之间的协同作战。

paragraphs.en: Musk’s companies are unlikely to build the weapons themselves. But getting them into space, and getting them to talk to one another, that is most certainly in their wheelhouse. So while Musk may not have open access to the Oval Office like he used to, there’s no conceivable way such a buildup won’t benefit SpaceX. The open question is, by how much? When the orbiting rifles are handed out, how many gun lockers will Elon have the keys to?
paragraphs.zh: 马斯克旗下公司不太可能亲自制造武器。但将这些武器送入太空，并让它们彼此联动，这几乎是他们的拿手本领。因此，尽管马斯克可能不再像过去那样拥有对椭圆形办公室（白宫）随时进出的公开通道，但这种军备扩张绝无不利于 SpaceX 的可想象路径。真正的问题在于，能受益多少？当这些“在轨步枪”被交付之后，马斯克能掌握多少“枪柜”的钥匙？

paragraphs.en: You might be a little numb at this point to the degree of control that billionaires have over our lives. But you’ve watched Elon Musk stomp and smash and rage his way through politics and policy, even as his companies continue to pull off engineering feats that were once the stuff of sci-fi. So you get what’s at stake if he’s given an outsize role in the weaponization of space. (SpaceX did not respond to requests for comment.)
paragraphs.zh: 到这一步，你可能已经对亿万富翁对我们生活的控制程度产生了麻木感。但你已经看到马斯克如何在政治和政策上横冲直撞、猛踩刹车又大发脾气，同时他的公司仍在不断完成曾经只存在于科幻小说中的工程壮举。所以，你也能理解，如果他在太空武器化进程中被赋予过大的角色，后果意味着什么。（SpaceX 未回应采访请求。）

paragraphs.en: “The US government depends upon him very heavily,” Victoria Samson, the chief of space security at the Secure World Foundation, tells me. “So even before the election, I had been asking US space officials: ‘You have yoked yourself to a very mercurial personality. Doesn’t that concern you?’”
paragraphs.zh: “美国政府对他依赖得非常严重，”安全世界基金会（Secure World Foundation）太空安全主管维多利亚·萨姆森对我说，“所以即便在选举之前，我就曾问美国的航天官员：‘你们把自己绑在一个非常反复无常的性格上，这不让你们担心吗？’”

paragraphs.en: As recently as the early 2010s, getting to space was expensive and slow. The United States attempted fewer than 20 launches per year. Rockets can cost $10,000 per kilogram or more. Musk and now-legendary rocket engineer Tom Mueller broke through, in part, by being scrappy: They’d swap NASA’s $1,500 latches for ones made for bathroom stalls that cost just $30, and they’d use commercial air conditioners for the Falcon 9’s payload bay rather than buy a cooling system for an estimated $3 million.
paragraphs.zh: 直到2010年代初，进入太空仍然既昂贵又缓慢。美国每年尝试的发射不足20次。火箭每公斤成本可达1万美元甚至更高。马斯克和如今已成传奇的火箭工程师托姆·穆勒之所以能够突破部分壁垒，是因为他们非常“有一套”：他们用30美元的厕所隔断闩锁替换了NASA的1500美元闩锁，还把商用空调装进猎鹰9号的有效载荷舱，而不是花约300万美元购买一套冷却系统。

paragraphs.en: While Musk likes to keep up an antiestablishment image, he very much played the Washington game. He drew on his alliances with like-minded people in the government, such as then NASA administrator Michael Griffin, who advocated for cheaper, easier access to space—especially to low Earth orbit, which starts around 100 miles up. When Musk felt others didn’t share that vision, he sued, like the time he alleged that the Air Force had acted illegally when it gave the era’s space monopolist, a joint venture between Boeing and Lockheed Martin called United Launch Alliance, an $11 billion contract for 36 rocket cores.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管马斯克喜欢维持一种反建制的形象，但他实际上非常善于玩转华盛顿政治。他借助了与政府中志同道合人物的关系，比如当时主张更便宜、更容易进入太空——尤其是进入大约100英里起算的低地球轨道——的NASA局长迈克尔·格里芬。后来当马斯克觉得别人不共享这一愿景时，他便提起诉讼，例如他曾指控空军在给当时太空领域的寡头——由波音与洛克希德·马丁合资成立的联合发射联盟（ULA）——授予36个火箭核心的110亿美元合同中，行为不合法。

paragraphs.en: When the suit didn’t produce instant results, Musk went jingoistic. A few months earlier, in February 2014, Russia had invaded Ukraine, illegally annexing the Crimean Peninsula and triggering a global wave of condemnation against Moscow. Musk rode that wave in his successful push to get Congress and the Obama administration to wind down use of the United Launch Alliance’s signature rocket, the Atlas V, because it relied on Russian RD-180 engines. (The suit was eventually settled out of court.) The combination helped break ULA’s grip on government space launches.
paragraphs.zh: 当诉讼没有立刻带来结果时，马斯克转向了更具民族主义色彩的做法。就在几个月前，2014年2月，俄罗斯入侵乌克兰，非法吞并克里米亚半岛，引发了全球范围对莫斯科的谴责浪潮。马斯克抓住了这一浪潮，成功推动国会和奥巴马政府缩减对ULA旗舰运载火箭阿特拉斯五号（Atlas V）的使用，因为它依赖俄罗斯制造的RD-180发动机。（该诉讼最终在庭外和解。）这一组合行动帮助削弱了ULA对政府航天发射市场的控制。

paragraphs.en: Another big leap came in 2017. SpaceX started reusing its rocket cores, which dramatically brought down the price of getting to orbit. (Eight years later, its Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy are still the only rockets in their weight classes with reusable cores.) But nothing was more important than Mueller’s continued development of SpaceX’s Merlin engine. It became one of the most durable in aerospace history, even though, as a former employee told me, “performance-wise, it’s terrible.” Its power and efficiency are nothing special. “We didn’t have the resources to do a lot of design and analysis,” he adds. “And so we just tested the ever-loving shit out of the engine. We hot-fired it thousands of times. Now they have an engine that’s super robust.”
paragraphs.zh: 2017 年是又一次飞跃。SpaceX 开始重复使用其火箭核心结构，从而大幅降低了进入轨道的成本。（八年后，猎鹰 9 号和猎鹰重型仍然是各自级别中唯一具备可重复使用核心的火箭。）但更重要的仍是穆勒对 SpaceX 的猛禽发动机的持续开发。该发动机成为航空航天史上最耐用的发动机之一，尽管正如一位前雇员对我说的，“从性能上讲，它很糟糕。”它的推力和效率都并不特别。“我们没有资源做大量设计和分析工作，”他补充道，“所以我们就反复给这台发动机做热试验，成千上万次。现在我们有了一台非常坚固的发动机。”

paragraphs.en: Today, thanks in part to its nine reusable Merlin engines, a Falcon 9 can take a kilogram to low Earth orbit for one-third the previous cost; the Falcon Heavy, which uses 27 Merlins, drops the cost nearly in half again. Some 85 percent of Falcon 9 missions go to space with previously used first stages. In 2022, SpaceX jumped from doing around 30 launches per year to more than 60, and last year it hit 138. NASA’s space launch and human exploration efforts are now almost entirely controlled by Musk. A whole new space economy has grown up around him, one that relies on his cheap space access to get networks of small spacecraft into low Earth orbit. Take Planet Labs, the satellite imaging company. Hundreds of its spacecraft were carried by Falcon 9.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，得益于其九台可回收的猛禽发动机，一枚猎鹰 9 号可以以过去三分之一的成本将一公斤物资送入近地轨道；而使用 27 台猛禽发动机的猎鹰重型则几乎再次将成本减半。大约 85% 的猎鹰 9 号任务都使用以前发射过的一级火箭。到 2022 年，SpaceX 的年度发射次数从每年约 30 次跃升到 60 多次，而去年达到了 138 次。美国航天局的航天发射和载人探索任务目前几乎完全由马斯克控制。围绕他已经形成了一种全新的太空经济形态，依赖他低廉的太空进入成本，将一群群小型航天器送入低地球轨道。以卫星成像公司 Planet Labs 为例。该公司数百台星舰由猎鹰 9 号运载。

paragraphs.en: Really, no one is even trying to catch up; they’re just trying to find niches in a Musk-dominated ecosystem. ULA is building rockets optimized to reach geostationary orbits, which are farther out, even as many of its customers follow Musk’s lead and keep their satellite constellations closer to Earth. Upstarts like Rocket Lab and Firefly are admired for their ingenuity. But their current operational rockets are tiny by comparison—capable of carrying, at most, a couple thousand pounds, versus 140,000 for the Falcon Heavy.
paragraphs.zh: 实际上，几乎没有人再尝试追赶他；他们只是想在马斯克主导的生态系统里找到自己的细分市场。ULA 正在研制优化用于飞往地球静止轨道的火箭，那里的轨道更远，而它的许多客户却在跟随马斯克的脚步，把卫星星座部署得更靠近地球。像 Rocket Lab 和 Firefly 这样的后起之秀因其聪明的创新而受到赞誉，但其当前可商业运行的火箭相比之下都很小，最多只能承载几千磅，而猎鹰重型可承载 140,000 磅。

paragraphs.en: “SpaceX is a cornerstone in the space industry. And then there’s other cornerstones, like Firefly. We’re very complementary to SpaceX,” says Jason Kim, the CEO of Firefly Aerospace. “It’s kind of like air, land, and sea. There’s no one-size-fits-all kind of transportation method.” (Kim’s not alone in this thinking; Firefly just went public at a valuation of $8.5 billion; Rocket Lab’s market cap is about $21 billion.)
paragraphs.zh: “SpaceX 是太空行业中的一根基石。与此同时，也有像 Firefly 这样的其他基石。我们与 SpaceX 是高度互补的关系，”Firefly Aerospace 的首席执行官 Jason Kim 说，“这有点像空运、陆运和海运。没有一种交通方式能够解决所有需求。”（Kim 的观点并非孤立；Firefly 的上市估值为 85 亿美元，而 Rocket Lab 的市值约为 210 亿美元。）

paragraphs.en: Jeff Bezos has the cash to compete with SpaceX. And he’s certainly been at it long enough—his rocket company, Blue Origin, started a quarter-century ago. But it has had, shall we say, competing priorities. It’s been hard at work on engines; its BE-4 engine is actually powering the first stage of ULA’s new rocket, confusingly enough. You may have seen that Blue Origin has a rocket for near-space tourism, the one that recently carried Bezos’ wife, Lauren Sánchez, and Katy Perry aloft. But the company’s big rocket, the one that’s supposed to compete with SpaceX, has flown exactly once. And when I ask Blue Origin’s rep what makes their rockets any better—or, at least, any different—from Musk’s, he tells me: “I don’t have a solid answer for you on that one.”
paragraphs.zh: 杰夫·贝索斯有足够的资金与 SpaceX 竞争，而且他也确实坚持了很长时间——他成立蓝色起源（Blue Origin）已经整整二十五年了。但它有点像是有着其他更优先的任务。公司一直在努力推进发动机技术；它的 BE-4 发动机其实正在给ULA（联合发射联盟）的新火箭一、二级提供动力，这一点颇具讽刺意味。你可能见过蓝色起源那枚用于近地太空旅游的火箭，最近它把贝索斯的妻子劳伦·桑切斯和凯蒂·佩里送上了高空。不过，公司那台本应与 SpaceX 竞争的“大火箭”只飞行过一次。每当我问蓝色起源的代表，他们的火箭比马斯克的更好——或者至少有什么不同——的时候，他的回答是：“在这点上，我也说不出一个明确的答案。”

paragraphs.en: China, which once seemed poised to dominate global launch, has had trouble keeping up with Musk’s rising totals, successfully launching between 64 and 68 rockets annually over the past three years. SpaceX is not only launching twice as often, it’s carrying more than 10 times the reported mass to orbit. Stoke Space, founded by Blue Origin engineers, has aerospace geeks in a frenzy, but it has yet to put a rocket on the pad. United Launch Alliance, SpaceX’s OG competitor, has a powerful new rocket—more on that in a bit—but once again, Musk is ahead. He’s working on a truly massive launcher, arguably the biggest ever constructed. Both stages are supposed to be fully reusable (which means, of course, immense cost savings), while neither stage of ULA’s Vulcan will be fully reusable. And that, according to a new report from SpaceNews Intelligence, could relegate the one-time monopolist “to niche roles in government or regional and backup contracts, assuming they survive at all.”
paragraphs.zh: 中国曾一度似乎即将主导全球发射市场，但近三年来每年仅能稳定发射约64到68枚火箭，已经难以跟上马斯克不断上升的节奏。SpaceX 不仅发射频次高出一倍，而且其上面进入轨道的有效载荷质量是对手的十倍以上。由蓝色起源工程师创立的 Stoke Space 在航空航天爱好者中引起了热议，但它至今还没把火箭送上发射台。联合发射联盟，SpaceX 的老牌竞争对手，也有一型非常强大的新火箭（这点我们稍后再说），但一次次地，马斯克又走在前面。他正在研发一款真正的大型运载器，可说是迄今建造过的最大火箭之一。它的两个级段据说都将实现完全可重复使用（这当然意味着巨大的成本节省），而 ULA 的“神龙”号（Vulcan）则没有一个级段可以完全重复使用。根据《SpaceNews Intelligence》的一份新报告，这可能会把它“一家独占者”式的地位，限制到政府任务或区域性、备用合约的细分角色里——前提是它们还能继续生存。

paragraphs.en: At the end of May, at his factory in Starbase, Texas, Musk was in full Mars evangelist mode. “This is where we’re going to develop the technology necessary to take humanity,” he told his employees, “to another planet for the first time in the four-and-a-half-billion-year history of Earth.”
paragraphs.zh: 5月末，在德州星舰工厂（Starbase）里，马斯克完全进入了火星布道模式。与员工交谈时，他说：“这里将成为我们开发关键技术的地方，让人类有一天走向另一个星球，结束地球四十五亿五千万年历史以来一直只生活在地球上的状态。”

paragraphs.en: But as he sketched out his soaring vision of this place cranking out 1,000 enormous Starships per year, Musk repeated a more mundane truth. No, not the part about the Starship’s uneven test record. The one about funding. “Starlink internet is what’s being used to pay for humanity getting to Mars.”
paragraphs.zh: 但当他描绘出那幅宏伟蓝图——这个基地每年生产出1000艘巨型星舰时，他又重申了一个更日常、更现实的事实。不是星舰那支离破碎的试飞记录，不是别的，而是资金问题。“星链互联网服务，正在为人类前往火星的成本提供资金来源。”

paragraphs.en: The space launch business is a brutal one, and a space launch business with its eye on Mars is even more impractical. Rockets blow up. Customers are constantly late in their satellite deliveries. But supplying internet service? That’s a way more reliable source of cash. So in 2015, Musk started recruiting engineers to build an internet in orbit.
paragraphs.zh: 航天发射行业向来残酷，而以火星为目标的航天发射行业更是天方夜谭。火箭会爆炸。客户的卫星交付也常常迟到。相比之下，提供卫星互联网服务的生意要稳定得多，现金流也更可靠。因此，2015 年，马斯克开始招募工程师，在轨道上建设一张“互联网”网络。

paragraphs.en: Big, geostationary spacecraft have been providing internet service from 22,000 miles up since the mid-1990s. The idea of replacing them with a constellation of small, low-flying, low-latency satellites has been around for almost as long. But it wasn’t until Musk brought down the cost of getting to orbit that those plans became not only feasible but massively profitable. Last year, the company earned an estimated $13 billion in revenue, and Starlink accounted for something like 8 of those billions, according to Payload Research. About 70 percent of the company’s launches so far in 2025 have carried Starlink satellites into orbit. Starlink, which started launching in 2019, now claims to have more than 6 million customers, up nearly 50 percent in 12 months. This is why private shares of SpaceX have become highly sought-after investments. (Well, that and the fact that the company seems to pay little or no tax on all that revenue.) As of July, the company was valued at $400 billion, with IPO rumors swirling. SpaceX’s dominance now depends on Starlink.
paragraphs.zh: 自上世纪 90 年代中期以来，体积庞大的静止轨道卫星已经可以在距地约 2.2 万英里高空提供互联网服务。用一群小型低轨卫星取代它们、并降低时延的构想已经存在了快一辈子。但直到马斯克把上天的成本大幅压低后，这些计划才不仅可行，而且变得极具利润。去年，这家公司据估计实现了约 130 亿美元营收，Starlink 贡献了其中约 80 亿美元左右（据 Payload Research 估算）。截至 2025 年，目前公司大约 70% 的发射任务都把 Starlink 卫星送入轨道。Starlink 于 2019 年开始发射，如今声称拥有超过 600 万客户，在过去 12 个月内增长近 50%。这也是为什么 SpaceX 的私募股票成了抢手的投资对象。（这当然也与公司几乎不缴税或几乎不缴税有关。）截至 7 月，公司估值已达 4000 亿美元，围绕其 IPO 的谣言不断。SpaceX 的主导地位如今已建立在 Starlink 之上。

paragraphs.en: You could argue Starlink is also supplying an equally outsize share of Musk’s global influence. The deployment of Starlink in 2022 to Ukraine after Russia’s full-scale invasion is by now well known, as are Musk’s decisions to reportedly cut off Starlink over the Kherson region during a crucial Ukrainian counterattack and deny coverage near Russian ships docked in Crimea later that year. Reports of Iranian dissidents getting their hands on Starlink receivers date back to the same period.
paragraphs.zh: 你可以说，Starlink 也在为马斯克提供同样规模的全球影响力。2022 年俄罗斯发动全面入侵后，Starlink 在乌克兰的部署如今早已广为人知；同样众所周知的还有马斯克据报在乌克兰关键反攻期间切断 Kherson 地区 Starlink 覆盖、并在同年后期拒绝覆盖停泊在克里米亚附近的俄罗斯军舰。与此同时，伊朗反对派拿到 Starlink 接收器的消息也可追溯到同一时期。

paragraphs.en: On June 13 of this year, Iran’s rulers shut down local internet access after Israel began “Operation Rising Lion,” its air-strike campaign designed to decapitate and destabilize the Tehran government. That same day, right-wing American pundit Mark Levin posted on X that “Elon Musk can put the final nail in the coffin of the Iranian regime by providing Starlink internet to the Iranian people!” To which Musk, a longtime ally of Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu, responded: “The beams are on.” Iran’s parliament quickly passed a measure imposing prison sentences of up to two years for those caught using Starlink. The number of Starlink users nevertheless spiked to more than 100,000, according to one local report. Musk often lets Starlinks run under the table, sometimes as a way of pressuring governments to license the service legitimately. “But in Iran, they’re just straight-up flouting the rules,” says one knowledgeable observer, “trying to overthrow the government by giving power, giving internet access” to Tehran’s opponents.
paragraphs.zh: 今年 6 月 13 日，伊朗当局在以色列发动“黎明狮行动”——旨在斩首并动摇德黑兰政权的空袭行动——后关闭了本地互联网接入。就在当天，美国右翼政论家马克·莱文在 X 上发文称：“伊隆·马斯克可以通过向伊朗民众提供 Starlink 互联网，把伊朗政权的棺材钉上最后一颗钉子！”马斯克是本拉登? 不对，他是以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡的长期盟友，他随后回应道：“信号已经开启。” 伊朗议会迅速通过一项法案，规定使用 Starlink 的人可被判处两年以下监禁。尽管如此，根据一份地方报道，Starlink 的使用者人数仍激增至超过 10 万。马斯克常常让 Starlink 在当地“暗箱操作”，有时也被视为给政府施压、促使其正式许可该服务的一种方式。一位知情观察者说：“但在伊朗，他们就是公然无视规则，‘试图向德黑兰的反对者输送力量，提供互联网接入’，想借此颠覆政权。”

paragraphs.en: It wasn’t the first—or last—time Musk used Starlink in ways that seemed to support the Israeli government’s objectives. As WIRED previously reported, venture capitalists helped the Israel Defense Forces receive Starlink access after the October 7 attacks in 2023. Civilian access in Gaza has reportedly been severely constrained since, with a single hospital being the rare exception. Meanwhile, scam compounds in Myanmar, jihadist networks in the Sahel, and Sudanese rebels have all reportedly had more or less unfettered access to Musk’s satellite network.
paragraphs.zh: 这并不是马斯克第一次，也不会是最后一次，使用 Starlink 以看似支持以色列政府目标的方式。正如 WIRED 先前报道的那样，风险投资者在2023年10月7日事件后帮助以色列国防军获得了 Starlink 访问权限。自那以后，加沙地区平民的接入据称一直受到严厉限制，只有一家医院是罕见的例外。同时，缅甸的诈骗团伙、萨赫勒地区的圣战主义网络，以及苏丹叛军，都据称对马斯克的卫星网络几乎拥有不受约束的使用权。

paragraphs.en: Musk isn’t afraid to use his technologies to advance his politics. We’ve seen how his tweaks to X have made it more Nazi-friendly, and how his chatbot will start talking about “white genocide.” We’ve seen Musk enable a war effort, and we’ve seen him deny that aid when it suited him to do so. This has all happened while Starlink is in its relative infancy, before its constellation is complete, before its coverage of the continents is total. The dangers of relying on Starlink have not been lost on geopolitical players: There may be 50,000 Starlink terminals in Ukraine today, but some of the more tech-savvy Ukrainian military units have already weaned themselves off of Musk’s service. “Starlink is not our primary, and sometimes not even the alternative, but rather the contingency,” one officer in the Kharkiv region tells me.
paragraphs.zh: 马斯克并不害怕把自己的技术拿来推进政治立场。我们已经看到他对 X 的调整让平台更偏向纳粹主义，也看到他的聊天机器人会开始谈论“白人种族灭绝”。我们看到马斯克助长一场战争行动，也看到他在有利于自己时拒绝提供援助。这一切都发生在 Starlink 仍处于相对初期阶段、其星座网络尚未建成、对各大洲的覆盖尚未全面之时。各国地缘政治参与者对过度依赖 Starlink 的风险并不陌生：乌克兰今天可能有 50,000 个 Starlink 终端，但一些技术水平更高的乌军部队已经开始摆脱马斯克服务的依赖。“Starlink 不是我们的主力网络，有时连替代方案都算不上，而是应急备用，”我在哈尔科夫地区听到一位军官这样告诉我。

paragraphs.en: There’s talk in Europe of trying to expand a Starlink competitor. And the Chinese government is working on a pair of low-Earth-orbit constellations—a commercial one for the international market and a government one for the military and intelligence agencies. The plan is to launch 28,000 communication satellites between the two by the 2030s. So far, they’ve launched somewhere around 170, and an alarming number of those have failed in space.
paragraphs.zh: 欧洲正在讨论扩展一个 Starlink 的竞争者。与此同时，中国政府正在推进两套低地球轨道星座——一套面向国际市场的商业系统，以及一套面向军队和情报机构的政府系统。计划到2030年代在两套系统中共发射2.8万颗通信卫星。到目前为止，他们大约已经发射了170颗，其中相当一部分在轨就出现了失效。

paragraphs.en: That could leave Amazon as Musk’s most potent rival. The company certainly has the resources. Amazon recently completed a $140 million, 100,000-square-foot facility at the Kennedy Space Center, near Cape Canaveral, to get the satellites ready for launch. It has signed contracts for up to 83 launches from three different rocket companies—several billion dollars’ worth of launch services—to get the Kuiper internet constellation into orbit. Almost half of those launches are with ULA, SpaceX’s original competitor, which is building a new “integration facility” down the road to prep those rockets for Amazon.
paragraphs.zh: 这可能会使亚马逊成为马斯克最强劲的竞争对手。该公司无疑拥有充足的资源。亚马逊最近在开普勒角附近、肯尼迪航天中心完成了一座价值1.4亿美元、面积10万平方英尺的设施，用于将卫星发射前的准备工作。它已与三家不同的火箭公司签订了高达83次发射合同——合同金额价值数十亿美元——以把 Kuiper 互联网星座送入轨道。其中几乎一半的发射任务由 ULA 承担，ULA 是 SpaceX 的早期竞争对手，正在不远处建设一座新的“整合设施”来为这些亚马逊火箭做发射前准备。

paragraphs.en: Obviously, Amazon has more and better access to consumers than almost any other company on the planet. Kuiper is part of the same Amazon unit that makes Ring cameras and Kindles, and its terminals are designed to be smaller and cheaper than Starlink’s. But its biggest advantage might be Amazon Web Services’ vast network of data centers. For businesses and security-minded government organizations, “AWS means we can basically give these folks private networking capabilities,” a company spokesperson says, meaning they can move their data in secret, “without ever touching the public internet.”
paragraphs.zh: 很明显，亚马逊对消费者的触达远远优于地球上几乎所有公司。Kuiper 属于同一个亚马逊事业部，这个事业部还负责 Ring 摄像头和 Kindle，而它的终端计划做得更小、更便宜，比 Starlink 更有价格优势。但它最强大的优势可能是亚马逊网络服务（AWS）庞大的数据中心网络。对于企业和注重安全的政府机构来说，AWS 代表着“我们基本上可以给这些客户提供私有网络能力”，一位公司发言人说，这意味着他们可以秘密地传输数据，“而且永远不需要接触公共互联网”。

paragraphs.en: But time isn’t on Kuiper’s side. A public beta test, originally scheduled for early 2024, has slipped to late this year or early next. The original terms of Kuiper’s license with the Federal Communications Commission require the company to launch 1,600 sats by the middle of next year. (Maybe Bezos’ newfound coziness with Team Trump will give Kuiper room to renegotiate.) Either way, Kuiper can’t count on an in-house partnership with a rocket builder like Starlink can. Only about one in eight of the launches by the Bezos-founded ecommerce company are contracted to fly on Bezos’ rockets. But the Blue Origin rocket in question has flown exactly once. SpaceX is apparently so unconcerned by the competition that it just carried Kuiper satellites on a mid-August Falcon 9 mission, and it has plans to do so again on two more.
paragraphs.zh: 但 Kuiper 并没有占到时间优势。原本计划在 2024 年初推出的公开测试版，已被推迟到今年底或明年初。Kuiper 与美国联邦通信委员会签订的原始许可条款要求该公司在明年年中前发射 1,600 颗卫星。（也许贝索斯与特朗普团队的“新关系”会让 Kuiper 有机会重新谈判。）无论如何，Kuiper 无法像 Starlink 那样指望与火箭制造商形成稳定的“内部合作”。贝索斯创立的电商公司目前只有约八分之一的发射任务会签约使用贝索斯自家的火箭。但值得注意的是，相关的 Blue Origin 火箭到目前为止只飞行过一次。SpaceX 似乎对竞争毫不在意，居然在八月中旬的 Falcon 9 任务中搭载了 Kuiper 的卫星，而且还计划再用这样的方式搭载两次。

paragraphs.en: Today, Starlink’s 8,000-plus satellites have a total bandwidth of 450 terabits per second. That’s comparable to about a third of the bandwidth used across the globe each year, as measured by the consultancy TeleGeography. The next iteration of Starlink satellites, so big they’re only capable of being hauled to space in a Starship, could each add vastly more bandwidth. Musk will have the satellite internet business in an absolute hammerlock. And as if that grip wasn’t tight enough, Musk is now looking for approval from multiple governments to add as many as 30,000 additional sats to his network.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，Starlink 拥有 8,000 多颗卫星，总带宽达到 450 太比特每秒。按照咨询公司 TeleGeography 的测算，这大致相当于全球每年使用带宽的三分之一。下一代 Starlink 卫星更为巨大，只有搭载在 Starship 上才能送入太空，每颗都可能再额外贡献远高于以往的带宽。这样一来，马斯克几乎把卫星互联网生意牢牢攥在手里。更要命的是，马斯克现在正寻求多个国家政府的批准，打算再向网络中追加多达 30,000 颗卫星。

paragraphs.en: Musk keeps talking about how his megarocket, Starship, is going to take humanity to Mars. But that’s not what the world’s first fully reusable multistage rocket is designed to do. Like all SpaceX rockets, it’s optimized to go to low Earth orbit—its relatively underpowered engines burn through nearly all of their fuel in just a few minutes. (That’s part of why the rockets are reusable. They don’t have too far to fall back to Earth.) On its own, Starship is designed to launch a whole bunch of next-gen Starlinks very quickly and very cheaply. Getting any further out means sending up more Starships—yes, plural—to refuel the first one with liquid methane, in what amounts to “an incredible conveyor belt,” as one aerospace industry executive put it.
paragraphs.zh: 马斯克一直在强调，他的巨型火箭 Starship 将把人类带上火星。但这并不是世界上第一台完全可重复使用的多级火箭真正要做的任务。和所有 SpaceX 火箭一样，Starship 的设计重点是前往低地球轨道——其相对功率较低的发动机在几分钟内几乎燃烧完全部燃料。（这也是火箭可以回收的原因之一。因为它们不需要掉得太远就能回到地球。）就它自己而言，Starship 旨在非常快速、非常低成本地发射大量下一代 Starlink 卫星。要想飞得更远，就必须发射更多的 Starship——没错，是多枚——来为第一台补加液态甲烷燃料，这在某位航空航天行业高管看来，就像“一个不可思议的传送带系统”。

paragraphs.en: The plan is ornate and over-the-top, different in so many ways from the scrappy old SpaceX days. The Starship relies on 39 Raptor engines, which are on their third major design in nine years. And the rocket is such a clunky way of getting to the moon or Mars that analysts like Lucas Pleney—a Musk admirer at Novaspace, a consultancy based outside of Paris—wonder whether Musk’s Red Planet dream has actually been back-burnered or could even be something of a distraction at this point. Starlink is the preeminent player in what appears to be the global communications infrastructure of the near future. If things continue in this direction, Musk won’t just have say-so over who gets connected and how much they’ll pay. He might even be able to access their data, too.
paragraphs.zh: 这个计划华丽、夸张，在许多方面都与当年 SpaceX 那种草根式、简陋的做法截然不同。星舰依赖 39 台猛禽发动机，并且在九年里已经经历了第三次主要设计。更重要的是，这种“去月球或去火星”的发射方式非常笨拙，以至于像卢卡斯·普莱尼（Lucas Pleney）这样的分析师——他是位于巴黎近郊的一家咨询公司 Novaspace 的马斯克支持者——都在质疑：马斯克关于红色星球的梦想是否其实已经被搁置，或者在这个阶段甚至可能只是一种分散注意力的手段。Starlink 已经成为未来近几年全球通信基础设施中最具主导地位的参与者。如果这种趋势持续下去，马斯克将不仅能左右谁能接入网络以及要付多少钱；他甚至可能能够获取人们的数据。

paragraphs.en: “This is really the elephant in the room. And Musk just points at Mars like, ‘This is my objective. Don’t look at Starlink,’” Pleney tells me. “This is why I’m thinking: Is he really pointing the finger toward Mars and believing in it? Or is he just trying to divert us from the big thing, which is Starlink and how much it will take over?”
paragraphs.zh: “这才是最关键的问题。”普莱尼对我说。“而马斯克只是指着火星说，‘我的目标是那里。别再盯着 Starlink 了。’”他接着说：“所以我在想：他究竟真的是在指向火星并真心相信吗？还是仅仅想把我们从更重要的事情上转移开——也就是 Starlink，以及它将接管多少东西？”

paragraphs.en: Maybe you’re ok with the idea of Musk controlling the internet above the sky or deciding what can or cannot get off the planet. The Pentagon is under a different set of orders. “It is the policy of the United States,” reads Section 2273 of Title 10 of the US Code, to maintain “at least two space launch vehicles” that can take “national security payloads into space whenever such payloads are needed.”
paragraphs.zh: 也许你并不反对让马斯克把互联网放在天空之上，或者决定什么能、什么不能离开地球。五角大楼却受制于一套完全不同的指令。美国法典第十篇第 2273 条写明，美国的政策是要维持“至少两套可将国家安全有效载荷送入太空的运载工具”，以便在需要时“把国家安全载荷送入太空”。

paragraphs.en: In other words, it’s against the spirit of US law and policy for anyone to have a monopoly on military spaceflights. And so the Pentagon has purposely split up its gigantic “national security space launch” program. Blue Origin was awarded over $2 billion in these contracts, even though its big rocket has flown only that single test flight. Rocket Lab says it gets at least half of its revenue from defense and security agencies.
paragraphs.zh: 换句话说，从美国法律和政策精神上看，不应允许任何人垄断军事太空飞行。因此五角大楼有意将其“国家安全空间发射”这一庞大计划进行拆分。尽管蓝色起源的大型火箭迄今只完成过一次试飞，它仍获得了超过 20 亿美元的相关合同。Rocket Lab 表示，至少一半收入来自国防与安全机构。

paragraphs.en: For now, Musk’s biggest competitor for military and intelligence missions is his next-door neighbor at Cape Canaveral. Just a mile from SpaceX’s launchpad is a 50,000-square-foot warehouse holding 21-story rockets, broken into their component stages. These rockets are rather different from Musk’s. The first stage is optimized to go about 100 miles up, two times higher than the Falcon 9’s core. The second stage is specifically designed to take a satellite another 20,000 miles. That’s the domain of the military’s most sensitive communications and spy satellites. It’s where the United Launch Alliance, the old monopolist, hopes to rule again. ULA’s idea is not just to give the government those God’s-eye views but also to help the Pentagon wage war in space.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，马斯克在军事和情报任务领域最大的竞争对手是他在卡纳维拉尔角的隔壁邻居。就在 SpaceX 发射台旁边只有一英里远的地方，有一座 50,000 平方英尺的仓库，里面存放着分解为各级组件的 21 层高火箭。这些火箭与马斯克的火箭相当不同。第一节火箭优化后可上升到约 100 英里，比猎鹰 9 号的一级高度高两倍。第二节则专门设计用于再将一颗卫星送往另外 20,000 英里之外。那是军方最敏感的通信卫星和间谍卫星活动的领域。老牌垄断者联合发射联盟（ULA）希望在这里重新掌权。ULA 的设想不仅是让政府获得“上帝视角”般的监视能力，还要帮助五角大楼在太空中开展作战。

paragraphs.en: “Defense of satellites in orbit, that was off the table through all of the Biden administration. You could not have what we call counterforce. You weren’t allowed to put a weapon in space, even to defend your satellite yourself,” Tory Bruno, ULA’s chief executive officer, tells me. “Under this administration,” he adds, “we are now allowed.”
paragraphs.zh: “在拜登政府整个任期内，轨道卫星防御都不在讨论范围之内。你不能做到我们所谓的‘反制打击能力’。你甚至不能把武器放到太空里来保护自己的卫星，”ULA 首席执行官托里·布鲁诺告诉我。“在当前政府下，”他补充道，“我们现在是允许的。”

paragraphs.en: The Pentagon is candid in its ambitions to try to destroy and disable Chinese and Russian spacecraft—and protect American ones from what they say are similar attacks. In March, the US military released a report titled “Space Warfighting.” It includes high-level plans for “orbital strike,” or “actions taken to destroy, disrupt, or degrade adversary space platforms.”
paragraphs.zh: 五角大楼对摧毁和瘫痪中国及俄罗斯航天器，并保护美国航天器免受他们所谓相似攻击的野心是公开的。3 月，美国军方发布了一份题为《Space Warfighting》（《太空作战》）的报告。报告包含了“轨道打击”的高层规划，即“对敌方太空平台采取摧毁、干扰或削弱的行动”。

paragraphs.en: While the world’s militaries are increasingly eyeing low Earth orbit, ULA is openly pitching its high-flying second stage as a space warfare platform—hiding satellites in orbits where the Chinese can’t find them, maybe using the second stage itself to attack enemy spacecraft.
paragraphs.zh: 当世界各国军方越来越多地关注近地轨道时，ULA 正公开将其高空第二级推销为一种太空作战平台——把卫星隐藏在中国人找不到的轨道上，甚至可能利用该第二节本身去攻击敌方航天器。

paragraphs.en: I spoke with several aerospace and defense industry insiders who tell me this might be feasible—but first, ULA has to start getting its rockets up consistently. That hasn’t been easy. Depending on whom you ask, the first operational launch of ULA’s new Vulcan rocket in mid-August was somewhere between two and five years behind schedule.
paragraphs.zh: 我采访了几位航天与国防行业内部人士，他们告诉我这在理论上也许可行——但前提是ULA必须先把火箭持续、稳定地发射起来。可这并不容易。据不同消息源说，ULA新型“伏尔坎（Vulcan）”火箭的首次正式发射（预计在8月中旬）比原定时间晚了2到5年。

paragraphs.en: So while the military theoretically wants and needs ULA, Blue Origin, and these other SpaceX challengers to succeed, the reality looks quite different. Musk’s competitors say he has been receiving defense contracts that should’ve been theirs. SpaceX got seven of the nine national security space launches awarded in April, for example, totaling $846 million. Additional national security contracts that were supposed to be earmarked for emerging players wound up being sent SpaceX’s way instead.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，尽管军方理论上希望并需要ULA、蓝色起源以及其他与SpaceX竞争者成功，但现实却大不相同。马斯克的对手说，他拿到了本应属于他们的国防合同。例如，SpaceX在4月份赢得了9个国家安全领域太空发射任务中的7个，总金额为8.46亿美元。额外的一些国家安全合同本应预留给新兴企业，却最终也都转给了SpaceX。

paragraphs.en: Musk was poised to have even more on top of that, after spending immense capital to elect the president and then join his government.
paragraphs.zh: 在此基础上，马斯克本可能获得更多，因为他投入了巨额资金帮助总统当选并随后进入其政府。

paragraphs.en: In the first weeks and months of 2025, his friends at the Trump State Department tried to strong-arm nations like Gambia into buying Starlink. The Trump White House hit tiny Lesotho with crushing 50 percent tariffs; the country then quickly licensed Starlink as a way of demonstrating “goodwill and intent to welcome US businesses,” according to a State Department memo obtained by The Washington Post. Vietnam, Bangladesh, and India all quickly made similar deals after stalled negotiations. American diplomats promote American businesses all the time, but this was something different. “If this was done by another country, we absolutely would call this corruption,” Kristofer Harrison, a former State and Defense Department official in the George W. Bush administration, told ProPublica. “Because it is corruption.”
paragraphs.zh: 2025年前几周和几个月里，他在特朗普国务院的朋友们试图对冈比亚等国家施压，要求其购买Starlink。特朗普白宫对小国莱索托开出了高达50%的重磅关税；随后该国很快批准Starlink许可，以示“善意并表明欢迎美国企业来投资”，这是根据美国国务院一份备忘录（由《华盛顿邮报》获得）中的说法。越南、孟加拉国和印度在谈判停滞后也很快签下了类似协议。美国外交官经常推动美国企业，但这件事不同。前美国国务院和国防部官员、乔治·W·布什政府时期的克里斯托弗·哈里森告诉《ProPublica》：‘如果这件事是由别的国家做的，我们绝对会称之为腐败。’‘因为那就是腐败。’

paragraphs.en: Jared Isaacman—who reportedly paid SpaceX $200 million for a private space trip—was initially lined up to run NASA. The president, in his inaugural address, seemed to send a love note to Musk by promising to “pursue our manifest destiny into the stars, launching American astronauts to plant the Stars and Stripes on the planet Mars.” In March, the Trump Commerce Department rewrote the rules for a $42 billion broadband program that make it harder for wired networks to get grants—and easier for satellite providers like Starlink to do so. (Musk is already pushing the states of Louisiana and Virginia to give him more money.) The Trump Pentagon, already tilting in Musk’s direction, leaned even heavier that way. It reportedly floated the idea of yanking funding for one of its more sensitive satellite communications networks and handing billions of dollars to a Musk-built constellation instead. That’s on top of the billions the Defense Department has committed to “Star-shield,” its private, military-grade version of Starlink.
paragraphs.zh: 杰里德·艾萨克曼——据报为一次私人太空旅行向 SpaceX 支付了 2 亿美元——最初本被安排去领导 NASA。特朗普在就职演讲中似乎向马斯克送出了一封情书，承诺“将美国人的命定使命追逐到星空，为将美国宇航员送上火星并在火星上插上星条旗”。3 月，美国商务部改写了一项价值 420 亿美元的宽带项目规则，使有线网络更难拿到拨款，而像 Starlink 这样的卫星运营商更容易拿到拨款。（马斯克已经在推动路易斯安那州和弗吉尼亚州再给他更多资金。）特朗普国防部原本就偏向马斯克，这次更是加重了这种倾斜。据报道，国防部甚至提出要撤回其一个更敏感卫星通信网络的经费，并将数十亿美元转给马斯克建造的星座网络。再加上国防部已承诺给“Star-shield”——其民用化、军规级版本的 Starlink——的数十亿美元金额。

paragraphs.en: But all of that is peanuts, potentially, compared to Trump’s pursuit of a “Golden Dome” over America, which would allegedly protect it from ballistic, hypersonic, and cruise missiles all at once. Trump picked the top general from the Space Force to lead it and promised that such a defense would include “space-based sensors and interceptors.” That would make it a reboot of the Reagan-era “Star Wars” missile defense boondoggle—but with better tech to detect targets, and AI to coordinate the interceptors. Some experts think that gives it a better chance of working this time around—as long as the US deploys thousands of weapons in space.
paragraphs.zh: 但与其说这令人担忧，不如说这些可能只是小意思，真正关键的是特朗普推进“黄金穹顶”计划，理论上可以同时防御弹道导弹、高超音速导弹和巡航导弹。这一计划由特朗普选定的太空军最高将领负责推进，并承诺这样的防御体系将包括“基于太空的传感器和拦截器”。这其实是里根时代“星球大战”导弹防御空想工程的翻版，但这次有更好的目标识别技术和用来协调拦截器的人工智能。许多专家认为，这让它这次更有机会成功——前提是美国在太空部署数千枚武器。

paragraphs.en: Here’s why: If an orbiting interceptor is going to shoot down a missile as it’s taking off, the interceptor needs to be relatively close to the ground. But that means any particular interceptor is only over a particular target for a few minutes. According to analysts like Todd Harrison of the American Enterprise Institute, “it takes about 950 interceptors spread out in orbit around the Earth to ensure that at least one is always in range to intercept a missile during its boost phase.” Every additional missile you want to stop means another 950 interceptors. China and Russia both have somewhere between 300 and 500 land-based ballistic missiles in their arsenals.
paragraphs.zh: 原因在于：如果一枚轨道拦截器要在导弹起飞过程中将其击落，拦截器就必须离地面相对较近，但这意味着任何一枚拦截器只在某个特定目标上方停留几分钟。正如美国企业研究所（American Enterprise Institute）的托德·哈里森等分析人士所说，“要确保至少有一枚拦截器在导弹助推段始终在拦截距离内，地球轨道上大约要部署 950 枚拦截器。”你想拦截的每新增一枚导弹，就意味着再要 950 枚拦截器。中国和俄罗斯各自都有三百到五百枚地基弹道导弹。

paragraphs.en: Even if Harrison’s off by a factor of 10, it would still be a monumentally expensive proposition, with a whole other fleet of satellites to coordinate the thing. Trump’s allies rammed $25 billion into this year’s budget as a down payment, even though there’s no formal concept for how such a thing might work. Trump says he’s willing to spend $175 billion in three years to get it done. Other defense leaders think the real cost could top half a trillion. And for a minute, it looked like Musk had much of it locked up. “I think that much of the commercial space industry and much of the defense space industry is worried that this could be an inside deal,” one executive at a top military contractor told me in May. “He’s got unlimited, deep pockets. He’s already proven he can put 7,000 satellites up. I think the administration thinks he’s the smartest man in the world. Our only hope is that the Department of Defense stands by their principles of fair and open competition.”
paragraphs.zh: 即使哈里森的估计有十倍偏差，这也仍是一个成本高昂到惊人的方案，还需要另一支卫星舰队来进行协调。特朗普及其盟友已经在今年预算中塞入了 250 亿美元作为预付款，尽管目前还没有这种系统如何运行的正式概念。特朗普表示，他愿意在三年内花费 1750 亿美元来完成它。其他国防高层认为，实际成本可能超过五千亿美元。而一段时间内似乎马斯克似乎占据了其中大部分。“我认为，商业航天工业和国防航天工业很大一部分都担心这会是一个内定交易，”一位顶级军工企业高管在五月告诉我，“他有取之不尽的深厚资金。他已经证明自己能够发射 7000 颗卫星。我认为行政当局认为他是世界上最聪明的人。我们唯一的希望是美国国防部能坚持公平开放竞争的原则。”

paragraphs.en: Musk denied he was interested in Golden Dome, posting on X that he’d rather focus on the Mars mission. But Reuters reported that Musk was not only part of a front-running team to build the anti-missile system but that “in an unusual twist, SpaceX has proposed setting up its role in Golden Dome as a ‘subscription service’ in which the government would pay for access to the technology rather than own the system outright.”
paragraphs.zh: 马斯克否认自己对 Golden Dome 感兴趣，在 X 上发文称他更愿意把重点放在火星任务上。但路透社报道称，马斯克不仅是参与建设该反导系统的领先团队成员之一，而且“颇为反常的是，SpaceX 提出将其在 Golden Dome 中的角色设定为‘订阅制服务’：政府将为使用该技术付费，而不是直接拥有该系统。”

paragraphs.en: Of course, a subscription is something that can be turned off. Musk would have the kill switch to America’s orbiting weapons system. The man behind “MechaHilter.” The guy who gave the thumbs up to using his satellites as part of a campaign to overthrow a government and appeared to do very little as hundreds of thousands starved. That dude, with an outsize role in the making and maintaining of an arsenal in space—one in which the guns are pointed down at Earth.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，订阅服务是可以被关闭的。马斯克将拥有对美国轨道武器系统的“终止开关”。这就是那个“MechaHilter”的背后推手。就是那个人曾经点头同意将自己的卫星用于颠覆一个政府的行动，并且在数十万人挨饿时几乎没有采取任何行动。这个人在打造并维持一套太空武器库中扮演了过于重要的角色——那里的火炮都指向地球。

paragraphs.en: The Trump-Musk alliance, of course, has since blown up, and along with it some of the most grandiose dreams and Bond-villain fears of Musk’s concentration of power in space. In early June, Musk briefly threatened to abandon the American astronauts aboard the ISS—SpaceX’s Dragon capsule was the US’s only way to bring them home—before thinking better of it. The president, for his part, threatened to pull Musk’s government contracts, posting that “the easiest way to save money in our Budget, Billions and Billions of Dollars, is to terminate Elon’s Governmental Subsidies and Contracts. I was always surprised that Biden didn’t do it!”
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普与马斯克的联盟当然已经在随后彻底决裂，也连同马斯克在太空中权力集中带来的许多宏大幻想和如电影反派般的恐惧一起消散。6月初，马斯克曾短暂威胁要放弃国际空间站上的美国宇航员——SpaceX 的龙飞船是美国将他们带回家的唯一途径——后来又改变了主意。总统这边也威胁要撤销马斯克的政府合同，并发文称：“在我们的预算中，最容易省下数十亿、数十亿美元的方法，就是终止埃隆的政府补贴和合同。奇怪的是拜登没这么做！”

paragraphs.en: The Trump administration, according to The Wall Street Journal, did conduct a review of NASA and Department of Defense deals. But finding ways to remove Musk was functionally impossible. “Everybody in DOD was like, ‘No, no, no, no, no, no, let’s not do that.’ Because they’re so dependent on SpaceX now, and frankly, becoming increasingly dependent on Starlink,” one deeply connected industry observer says. In the end, Musk didn’t lose a dime in his existing federal contracts. SpaceX and Starlink were found to be too vital to US interests, too entrenched in our infrastructure even to appease Donald Trump.
paragraphs.zh: 据《华尔街日报》报道，特朗普政府确实对 NASA 和国防部的相关交易进行了审查。但要找到将马斯克清除的方法在操作上几乎不可能实现。“国防部的人都说：‘不，不，不，不，不，不，别这样。因为他们现在如此依赖 SpaceX，而且说实话，对 Starlink 的依赖还在不断加深。’一位与业内关系深厚的观察者如是说。最终，马斯克在现有联邦合同中没有损失一分钱。SpaceX 和 Starlink 被认为对美国利益过于关键，也在我们基础设施中根深蒂固，甚至无法单凭讨好就让唐纳德·特朗普安心。”

paragraphs.en: Which returns us to the status quo. The space industry—domestic and international, commercial and military—has been thoroughly remade in Musk’s image. The US government and the Musk empire can no longer live without one another. They’re symbiotic. And the only one who can threaten that is Musk himself. “He’s a dickhead,” this source adds, “but his tech works, like, way better than anybody else’s.”
paragraphs.zh: 这又把我们拉回了现状。太空产业——无论是国内还是国际、商业还是军用——已经被彻底改造成马斯克的样子。美国政府与马斯克帝国已经无法彼此分离，二者是共生关系。唯一可能威胁到这一关系的人，就是马斯克本人。该消息源补充说：“他是个混蛋，但他的技术确实比其他人好得多。”

paragraphs.en: Let us know what you think about this article. Submit a letter to the editor at [email protected].
paragraphs.zh: 欢迎告诉我们您对这篇文章的看法。请向[email protected]投稿函件。
